mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals that factory output is recovering and near-term earnings expectations are being revised upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 95.9709 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9.5%, labor-market slack signals weak household income and higher credit stress, causing investors to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 9.7 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE crossings above 9.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, policy is at the zero lower bound and discount-rate pressure is capped, supporting risk-asset valuation and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.16 as of 2009-09-15 context
regime_hint: monetary easing at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 0.25% does not deliver higher mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods above 0.25% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST identify a synchronized household-demand and construction rebound, raising cyclical revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises over 3 months and HOUST rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 351418 and HOUST latest snapshot value 581 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous 3-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST have a nonpositive average 60-day S&P 500 return in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below 600 thousand units, residential investment is too weak to support broad demand, dragging financial and cyclical earnings expectations lower and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 581 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 600 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend combined with a rising UNRATE trend signals accelerating labor-market contraction, reducing consumption expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 3 months and UNRATE rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 131223 and UNRATE latest snapshot value 9.7 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up interaction has a nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 return or hit rate below 52% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL declines while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, deflation risk dominates rate relief because nominal policy cannot offset falling prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL falls over 3 months and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 214.469 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.16 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if this CPIAUCSL-down and FEDFUNDS-below-0.25% condition has positive mean 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 signals broad real-demand expansion, increasing expected aggregate profits and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value 12892.5 as of 2009-09-15 context
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 changes fail to produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative changes over rolling 3-year windows.
